criteria.label: State the SGD update rule and explain what makes the gradient stochastic.
criteria.example: Write θ ← θ − η∇L_B(θ) and note that B is a random mini-batch, so ∇L_B differs each step while averaging to the full gradient in expectation.

criteria.label: Implement a shuffle-and-batch SGD loop over a small dataset.
criteria.example: Train linear regression on 200 points with batch_size=32: permute indices each epoch, loop slices, compute mean squared error gradient, update w with lr=0.1 for 10 epochs.

criteria.label: Relate batch size to updates per epoch and gradient noise.
criteria.example: For N=8000 and B=32, compute ~250 updates per epoch and explain that halving B doubles updates but increases gradient variance.

criteria.label: Wire PyTorch DataLoader and SGD optimizer in a correct training step sequence.
criteria.example: Use DataLoader(shuffle=True), then for each batch: zero_grad → forward → loss → backward → optimizer.step(), and evaluate validation loss after each epoch.
